snippet.title: Tampa police chief during traffic stop: ‘I’m hoping that you’ll just let us go' | USA TODAY #Shorts
snippet.description:
The police chief of Tampa, Florida, has resigned following an incident last month in which she flashed her badge to get favorable treatment during a traffic stop while riding in a golf cart. 

RELATED: Florida deputies mistake legally blind man's cane for gun, arrest him https://bit.ly/3uoy9hJ

“It is unacceptable for any public employee, and especially the city's top law enforcement leader, to ask for special treatment because of their position,” Castor said in a statement. Chief Mary O’Connor stepped down upon the mayor’s request following an internal review, according to Castor. 

The incident took place on the evening of Nov. 12, when O’Connor and her spouse were riding in a golf cart on streets of Oldsmar, Florida, near Tampa. A Pinellas County Sheriff deputy pulled the couple over because the golf cart did not have a license plate. The deputy’s body camera captured footage of the interaction, which was posted by the Tampa Police Department. 

O’Connor, sitting in the passenger seat, asks the deputy “Are you recording,” and then tells him “I’m the police chief in Tampa ... I’m hoping that you’ll just let us go tonight,” as she shows him her badge. “You looked familiar,” the deputy responds, before letting the couple go.

» Subscribe to USA TODAY: http://bit.ly/1xa3XAh
» Watch more on this and other topics from USA TODAY: https://bit.ly/3QYKjbc  
» USA TODAY delivers current local and national news, sports, entertainment,  finance, technology, and more through award-winning journalism, photos, videos and VR.

#Florida #Police #CaughtonCamera
tags: tampa police chief mary o’connor resigns
tampa pd chief mary o connor

snippet.title: Free Will is Political
snippet.description:
Free Will – Our Freedom to choose for ourselves – is at the heart of our sense of being human. How we think about free will effects everything from responsibility and criminal justice to laziness and poverty to seemingly ordinary choices like what I’ll have for dinner. 

Free Will is of course the power to select from options, for ourselves, unencumbered, unrestrained, uncaused – to be the author of our own thoughts and actions. 

But what does this really mean? Does Free Will really exist? And is it a wider social, cultural, and political concept? Is it really about responsibility? I look at a few philosophers - P.F. Strawson, Spinoza, Plato, Socrates, and more - to explore the concept

Then & Now is FAN-FUNDED! Support me on Patreon and pledge as little as $1 per video: http://patreon.com/user?u=3517018

TFP - Ep. 24: Galway-Armagh insanity, the brawl and the gouge, Horan walks, Dublin, Kerry and Derry
snippet.description:
Welcome to Ep. 24 of The Football Pod 2.0 - James O'Donoghue and Paddy Andrews are back talking Football with Tommy Rooney. Timestamps below.

(00:30) – James' makes his live radio debut in Croke Park
(02:30) – The magic and insanity of Galway-Armagh
(15:30) – The brawl, the gouge and what the GAA need to do.
(34:50) – How Galway beat Armagh as the b-side shine.
(46:00) – Derry hammer Clare, weak points flare up.
(54:30) – Dublin-Kerry excitement, goal as key as Clifford and Con.
(01:08:50) – James Horan leaves Mayo - his legacy and the fallout
(01:21:30) – TFP Questions: Clutch frees, Sean O'Shea, Bernard Dunne in Galway.

Subscribe to the podcast in the 'OTB GAA' feed or in 'The Football Pod' feed. Brought to you in partnership with AIB - proud sponsors of the GAA Senior Football Championship. 

Check out #TheToughest for more.

We want to hear your questions too - get in touch on twitter or instagram or email: thefootballpod@offtheball.com

snippet.title: CityLab 2022
snippet.description:
We're live in Amsterdam for #CityLab2022. 

The conference, held in partnership with the Aspen Institute, focuses on the leadership role of cities on the global stage, bringing together mayors from around the world alongside prominent city innovators, business leaders, urban planners, and artists, to uncover, celebrate, and spread some of the boldest thinking and most actionable urban solutions addressing climate change, public health, refugees, and more.

Tune in live from 09:00 AM CET/3:00 AM EST. 

Morning Plenary 

00:00 Holding Screen 
12:12 How Amsterdam Uses Data to Innovate 
14:30 Dan Porterfield, President and CEO, Aspen Institute, Delivers Remarks 
19:49 Facing History to Forge a Brighter Future: London Breed, Mayor of San Francisco, California, U.S.A, Piotr M.A. Cywiński, Director, Auschwitz-Birkenau State Museum, Nancy Jouwe, Cultural Historian and Dan Porterfield, President and CEO, Aspen Institute
38:30 Crypto and Your City: Tonantzin Carmona, David M. Rubenstein Fellow, Brookings Institution, Mark Foster, Managing Director, Strategic Advisory Management and Stacy-Marie Ishmael, Managing Editor for Crypto, Bloomberg News
58:10 The Mastaba: Christo and Jeanne-Claude's Final Project: Vladimir Yavachev, Project Director, Christo and Jeanne-Claude Foundation
01:10:58 From Science Fiction to City Planning: Cities and "The Metaverse": Sean Audain, Strategic Planning Manager, Wellington City Council, New Zealand, Salla Eckhardt, Director of Digital Building Lifecycle Innovation, Microsoft and Linda Poon, Staff Writer, Bloomberg CityLab
01:26:40 City Stories: LAGOS: Peju Alatise, Artist with Baratunde Thurston, Host, “How to Citizen” Podcast
01:36:41 What Is a Mayor's Wartime Duty?: Anatoliy Fedoruk, Mayor of Bucha, Ukraine, Andriy Sadovyi, Mayor of Lviv, Ukraine and Natalie Jaresko, Chairperson of Aspen Institute Kyiv

Afternoon Plenary 

01:54:53 Holding Screen 
04:12:09 Prioritizing Public Health in a Post Pandemic World: Kostas Bakoyannis, Mayor of Athens, Greece, Chilando Chitangala, Mayor of Lusaka, Zambia and Dan Kass, Senior Vice President for Environmental, Climate, and Urban Health, Vital Strategies
04:30:40 Immersive Nature in Cities:  Amanda Burden, Principal of Urban Planning, Bloomberg Associates, Andreas Kipar, Founder, CEO, and Creative Director, Landscape Architecture Nature Development and Nuala McGovern, Broadcaster, BBC News
04:49:06 Chief Data Officers Meet the Moment: Justin Elszasz, Chief Data Officer, City of Baltimore, Maryland, U.S.A., Grace Simrall, Chief of Civic Innovation and Technology, City of Louisville, Kentucky, U.S.A., Ming Tang, Chief Data and Analytics Officer, National Health Services, England and Beth Blauer, Associate Vice Provost for Public Sector Innovation, Johns Hopkins University
05:06:30 Innovation Capital: Marta Cotrim, Head of Data and Smart City Unit for the City of Cascais,
Portugal, Lamia Kamal-Chaoui, Director, OECD Centre for Entrepreneurship, SMEs, Regions and Cities, Matteo Lepore, Mayor of Bologna, Italy, Darach O’Connor, Executive Manager, City of Dublin, Republic of Ireland and Sarah Holder, Writer, Bloomberg CityLab
05:25:49 Tourism Reset: Building a Vibrant and Sustainable Visitor Economy for All: Enver Duminy, CEO, Cape Town Tourism, Norbert Kettner, Managing Director, Vienna Tourist Board, Geerte Udo, CEO, Amsterdam and Partners and Lebawit Lily Girma, Travel News Reporter, Bloomberg Travel
05:44:59 Dynamic Capabilities in the Public Sector: Mariana Mazzucato, Professor in the Economics of Innovation and Public Value, University College London (UCL); Founding Director, UCL Institute for Innovation & Public Purpose
05:49:34 How Mexico City is Leading the World in Going Digital: Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo, Mayor of Mexico City, Mexico with James Anderson, Head of Government Innovation, Bloomberg
Philanthropies

About Bloomberg Philanthropies 
Bloomberg Philanthropies invests in 941 cities and 173 countries around the world to ensure better, longer lives for the greatest number of people. The organization focuses on five key areas for creating lasting change: the Arts, Education, Environment, Government Innovation, and Public Health. Bloomberg Philanthropies encompasses all of Michael R. Bloomberg’s giving, including his foundation, corporate, and personal philanthropy as well as Bloomberg Associates, a pro bono consultancy that works in cities around the world. In 2021, Bloomberg Philanthropies distributed $1.66 billion. 

Subscribe to the Bloomberg Philanthropies YouTube Channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCTBGx5nppO4o_fOy7hqgyMg 
 
More from Bloomberg ​Philanthropies:
Twitter: https://twitter.com/BloombergDotOrg 
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/bloombergdotorg/
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/bloombergdotorg 
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/bloomberg-philanthropies/
Website: https://www.bloomberg.org/

#BloombergPhilanthropies

snippet.title: Another Day In Paradise - The #Truth behind the #Lyrics
snippet.description:
Single by Phil Collins
from the album ...But Seriously

Released 1989
Genre Soft rock

Phil Collins’ 1989 hit “Another Day in Paradise” is a song that speaks of the harsh realities of homelessness and poverty, and the need for compassion and understanding. Collins wrote the song himself, inspired by the plight of the homeless he observed on the streets of London and New York.

The song starts with a simple drum beat and a haunting, minimalistic synth melody. The lyrics of the song are bleak and frank, as Collins reflects on the difficult life of the homeless, who struggle to survive each day without the basic necessities of life. He sings: “She calls out to the man on the street / ‘Sir, can you help me? / It’s cold and I’ve nowhere to sleep / Is there somewhere you can tell me?”

The song continues to explore the difficulties of being homeless, as Collins sings of the lack of resources and the feelings of hopelessness and despair. He sings of the need for kindness and understanding, and the need to take action to help those in need.

The song is also notable for its use of a vocal effect called ‘reverse reverb’, which gives the song a ghostly, echoing quality. This adds to the song’s atmosphere of loneliness and sadness.

The song was a huge hit, reaching number one in both the US and the UK, and earning Collins a Grammy for Record of the Year. The song has endured as an anthem for those who are struggling in society and a reminder of the need to take action to help those in need. Collins has described the song as “a plea for attention to the homeless problem.”

The song has been covered by many artists, it remains one of Phil Collins’s best-known and most beloved songs. It is a powerful reminder of the plight of the homeless and of the need for compassion and understanding.

The #Truth behind the #Lyrics:

Since its release in 1989, Phil Collins’ “Another Day in Paradise” has become a timeless classic. From its haunting opening instrumental to its powerful chorus, this song has captured the hearts of many. However, the lyrics of this song are often overlooked and misunderstood. While the song may be seen as a romantic ballad, its true meaning is much deeper.

The lyrics of “Another Day in Paradise” tell the story of a homeless person struggling to make it through the day. Collins paints a vivid picture of a person facing a life of poverty and hardship. He sings of a person who is “crouching on the edge of the street” and “longs to be warm”. These words capture the plight of homelessness and the desperation of those who are forced to live on the streets.

The song also speaks to the issue of social inequality. Collins sings of how the homeless person “finds it hard to see any chance of life changing”. He makes it clear that poverty is an issue of systemic injustice and not just an individual’s fault. The song highlights the reality that poverty is not simply a matter of luck but is often the result of structural inequality.

The final verse of the song provides a powerful call to action. Collins implores us to “show some understanding” and to “reach out a hand” to those less fortunate. He encourages us to step up and do something to help those in need. This message of compassion and action is one that resonates deeply today.

In conclusion, “Another Day in Paradise” is much more than a romantic ballad. The lyrics of the song tell the story of a homeless person struggling to get by and provide a powerful call to action. The message of compassion and action is one that is as relevant today as it was when the song was first released.

*****************
Dear viewers,

Thank you for watching and reading my content on YouTube. I'm grateful for your support and hope that you have enjoyed what I have put out. 

I'd like to invite you to participate further in my content by leaving comments, liking, and subscribing to my channel. This will help me to create more content and reach more people. It would also be greatly appreciated if you could help spread the word by sharing my content to your friends and family.

I also kindly ask for your help in protecting my content from being copied without my name attached. By notifying me of any such cases, you can help ensure that my content remains original and credited.

Once again, thank you for your support and I look forward to hearing your feedback and thoughts on my content.

Best regards,
Ulfa Syabania
*****************

Hello everyone! Thank you so much for listening to "Another Day In Paradise" by Phil Collins. I hope this song has touched your heart and made you think about the struggles of people living in poverty. This is a reminder to all of us that we have a responsibility to help those in need. Let's use this song to motivate and inspire us to make a difference in our world. Thank you for your time and support.

The lyrics of “Another Day in Paradise” tell the story of a homeless person struggling to make it through the day. Collins paints a vivid picture of a person facing a life of poverty and hardship. He sings of a person who is “crouching on the edge of the street” and “longs to be warm”. These words capture the plight of homelessness and the desperation of those who are forced to live on the streets.

The song also speaks to the issue of social inequality. Collins sings of how the homeless person “finds it hard to see any chance of life changing”. He makes it clear that poverty is an issue of systemic injustice and not just an individual’s fault. The song highlights the reality that poverty is not simply a matter of luck but is often the result of structural inequality.

The final verse of the song provides a powerful call to action. Collins implores us to “show some understanding” and to “reach out a hand” to those less fortunate. He encourages us to step up and do something to help those in need. This message of compassion and action is one that resonates deeply today.

In conclusion, “Another Day in Paradise” is much more than a romantic ballad. The lyrics of the song tell the story of a homeless person struggling to get by and provide a powerful call to action. The message of compassion and action is one that is as relevant today as it was when the song was first released.

*****************
Dear viewers,

Thank you for watching and reading my content on YouTube. I'm grateful for your support and hope that you have enjoyed what I have put out. 

I'd like to invite you to participate further in my content by leaving comments, liking, and subscribing to my channel. This will help me to create more content and reach more people. It would also be greatly appreciated if you could help spread the word by sharing my content to your friends and family.

I also kindly ask for your help in protecting my content from being copied without my name attached. By notifying me of any such cases, you can help ensure that my content remains original and credited.

Once again, thank you for your support and I look forward to hearing your feedback and thoughts on my content.

Best regards,
Ulfa Syabania
*****************

Hello everyone! Thank you so much for listening to "Another Day In Paradise" by Phil Collins. I hope this song has touched your heart and made you think about the struggles of people living in poverty. This is a reminder to all of us that we have a responsibility to help those in need. Let's use this song to motivate and inspire us to make a difference in our world. Thank you for your time and support.

snippet.title: Utah Treasurer Marlo Oaks on ESG Ratings
snippet.description:
Source:
https://www.podbean.com/eau/pb-qdcmq-12dd1e3

You may have read or heard strong opinions about ‘ESG’ in the news this year. 
What is it? And why is the State of Utah involved in a legal battle about it? As Utah’s official law firm, our attorneys are participating in Utah’s stand against Environmental Social Governance (ESG), based on its potential effect on taxpayers and the financial health of our state. We ask State Treasurer Marlo Oaks: What is ESG and why is it important?

snippet.title: Argentina Lithium & Energy Corp. (TSX-V: LIT, FSE: OAY3, OTC: PNXLF) - RICH TV LIVE Podcast
snippet.description:
About Argentina Lithium
Argentina Lithium & Energy Corp is focused on acquiring high quality lithium projects in Argentina and advancing them toward production in order to meet the growing global demand from the battery sector. The management group has a long history of success in the resource sector of Argentina and has assembled a first-rate team of experts to acquire and advance the best lithium properties in the "Lithium Triangle". The Company is a member of the Grosso Group, a resource management group that has pioneered exploration in Argentina since 1993.

Argentina Lithium & Energy Corp. (TSX-V: LIT, FSE: OAY3, OTC: PNXLF) - RICH TV LIVE Podcast - #argentinalithium #news #richtvlive #stocks #education #lithium #electicvehicles #ev #richtv #podcast #business #finance #mining #lit #pnxlf #win 

Argentina Lithium & Energy Corp. (TSX-V: LIT, FSE: OAY3, OTC: PNXLF), (“Argentina Lithium” or the “Company”) reports positive lithium brine values from the third and fourth diamond drill holes at its Rincon West Project in Salta Province, Argentina. The Rincon West project covers 3742.8 hectares of the salar basin, located west and north of the adjacent Rincon Project owned by Rio Tinto. The property is currently permitted for up to 9 exploration drill holes. The fifth exploration hole is currently in final steps to completion. The brine analytical results from the third and fourth holes have been received and are reported below.

“The fourth exploration hole has produced our best results to date. Lithium brines start at 38 metres depth, but the exciting result is the interval from 95 m to 227 m, with lithium values ranging from 334 to 382 mg/litre over a continuous 132 m interval.  The third hole, located in the southern portion of the property produced lower grade brines at the bottom of the hole. Though the lithium potential appears to decrease in the southern portion of the basin, it is notable that we still produce lithium brines while stepping out 1.8 km south from our previous drilling. We have more drilling to do, to fully delineate our brine aquifer, particularly in the west and north.” stated Miles Rideout, V.P. of Exploration.
